chunks.page_summary: This page provides a guide for integrating Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) sensors, specifically SRV and SRD models, with DataFEED OPC Server Modbus using Modbus TCP/IP protocol, including prerequisites, a communication diagram, and initial configuration steps.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Modbus TCP/IP Integration with DataFEED OPC Server Modbus

Modified on: Sun, 15 Feb, 2026 at 12:51 AM

Which Rheonics products are related to this Article?
This Article is based on the use of the SME (Smart Module Electronics) from SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/)...

What is this Article helpful for?
This article helps to guide the user on how to correctly receive data from the SME into the DataFEED OPC Software via Modbus TCP Protocol.

DataFEED OPC Server Modbus (https://industrial.softing.com/products/opc-opc-ua-software-platform/opc-server-middleware/datafeed-opc-suite-base.html)
This software solution enables communication between OPC devices and the cloud. It provides access to the most commonly used PLCs in the industry. The software collects data from a variety of sources, such as OPC UA servers, PLCs, classic servers or SQL databases. As soon as the data is collected, users can decide where it should be sent.

This software uses the terms Data Source and Data Destination to identify who defines and uses data items. This doesn't define the communication direction, which is bidirectional.

Prerequisites:
* DataFEED software installed
* Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) with Ethernet and Modbus TCP enabled
* Ethernet on SME should be set to a Static IP through RCP
* IP address of the Rheonics SME - this can be read out from the SME using RCP software

Ethernet Connection

Figure 1: Communication diagram for Rheonics sensor-PLC and PC.

Configuration steps to receive SME data through DataFEED OPC Server Modbus

1. Open DataFEED software, the software window should look like this:
key_facts: The article describes the Modbus TCP/IP integration of Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) with DataFEED OPC Server Modbus.
Rheonics products like SRV (inline viscometer) and SRD (inline density meter) utilize the SME.
The DataFEED OPC Server Modbus software facilitates communication between OPC devices/PLCs and the cloud, gathering data from various sources.
DataFEED software employs 'Data Source' and 'Data Destination' for data items, supporting bidirectional communication.
Key prerequisites include DataFEED software installation, Ethernet and Modbus TCP enablement on the Rheonics SME, setting a Static IP for SME Ethernet via RCP, and knowing the SME's IP address.
A communication diagram illustrates the connection between Rheonics sensor (SME-DRM), an Ethernet switch, and MODBUS clients/servers (PC, PLC).
diagram_labels: Figure 1: Communication diagram for Rheonics sensor-PLC and PC.
warnings_notes: For proper integration, DataFEED software must be installed.
Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) must have Ethernet and Modbus TCP enabled.
The Ethernet on the SME should be configured with a Static IP address using RCP software.
The IP address of the Rheonics SME needs to be known and can be retrieved using RCP software.
The document was last modified on Sun, 15 Feb, 2026 at 12:51 AM.
Contact phone number for Rheonics is +1 713 234 0863.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step guide on configuring Modbus data sources within the Softing dataFEED OPC Suite software, illustrating the selection process and parameter setup.

screenshot_details.window_title: Configuration1 - Softing dataFEED OPC Suite - Running (Demo Mode)

content_area.description: Create and modify connections to controllers and devices.

figure_2_context.caption: Figure 2: Data Source selection tree.
figure_2_context.instruction_point_2: 2. On the left side, you will find a navigator with possible data sources, select “Modbus”.

screenshot_details.window_title: Configuration1 - Softing dataFEED OPC Suite - Running (Demo Mode)

content_area.description: Create and modify connections to controllers and devices.

figure_3_context.caption: Figure 3: Modbus communication parameters for SME device.
figure_3_context.explanatory_text: Data sources from external devices will appear in the Controllers and Devices section. A unique name is assigned to each communication method in the Name Column. Listed in the second and third columns are the Type (Modbus) and Address (IPv4, hostname, or IPv6). The Parameters column displays connection attributes.
figure_3_context.instruction_point_3: 3. If there aren’t any previous configurations, it will be blank like the following image. To create a new data source, press “Add a new data source” (Ctrl+A).

key_facts: The Softing dataFEED OPC Suite software facilitates the configuration of various data sources, including Siemens, Rockwell, Modbus, B&R, Mitsubishi, OPC UA Server, and OPC Server.
The 'Controllers and Devices' section within the software is used for creating and modifying connections to external devices.
Each communication method in the 'Controllers and Devices' section is assigned a unique name.
Data source configurations display the Type (e.g., Modbus) and Address (IPv4, hostname, or IPv6).
The 'Parameters' column provides connection attributes such as Port, Protocol, and Read Timeout.
If no previous configurations exist, the 'Controllers and Devices' section will be blank.
To create a new data source, users can press “Add a new data source” (Ctrl+A).
diagram_labels: Figure 3: Modbus communication parameters for SME device.

chunks.page_summary: This document provides a step-by-step guide for configuring a Modbus TCP data source connection within the Softing dataFEED OPC Suite software, detailing how to set connection name, activate the connection, and specify communication parameters like IP address and port.
chunks.extracted_text:
Configuration1 - Softing dataFEED OPC Suite - Running (Demo Mode)
Configuration View Local Application Help
dataFEED®
Data Source - Modbus

  Modbus
  B&R

Settings
Data Source - Modbus.

Controllers and Devices
Create and modify connections to controllers and devices.
Name Type Address Parameters

Figure 4: Create a data source configuration.

4. When click-in on the “Add a new data source” button, the Controller/Device Connection Wizard will appear. The connection name can be assigned in this page. Set the connection as “Active” with the flag up, and deactivate the simulation option, click next.

Controller / Device Connection Wizard

Connection Settings
On this wizard page the Connection Settings of the actual data source connection to a Modbus device are configured.
Connection: Modbus_1.
Connection Name
Specify the name of the actual connection. This name is also always the name of the root node of the name space and must therefore unique throughout the whole configuration.
Connection name: Rheonics_1
Connection Settings
Specify if the connection shall be active or shall be in simulation mode.
Connection Active: [checked]
Simulation: [unchecked]
Application Protocol
Select the type of the protocol which shall be used at application layer.
Modbus TCP [selected]

Next > Cancel Help

Figure 5: Connection settings.

5. In the next window the SME IP Address must be entered. This IP address can be viewed or configured using the RCP software. The address format and advanced options can be left as shown. Then click the Next button.

Controller / Device Connection Wizard

Communication Settings
On this wizard page the parameters for the current data source connection are configured.
Connection: Rheonics_1 - Protocol: Modbus TCP.
Communication Settings
Specify the address of the current TCP/IP connection.
The address can be specified as an IP address V4, V6 or a hostname.
Use the Modify button for more advanced configuration options.
Destination Address: 10.5.33.84
Address format: Valid IPv4 address detected.
Advanced:
Write Allowed Yes
Read/Write Port 502
Type Client(Active)
Protocol TCP
Modify

< Back Next > Finish Cancel Help

Figure 6: Communication settings.
key_facts: The Softing dataFEED OPC Suite allows users to create and modify connections to various controllers and devices.
A "Controller/Device Connection Wizard" guides the user through the configuration process for new data sources.
Each connection must be assigned a unique name, which acts as the root node of the name space.
Connections can be set to "Active" mode, and a "Simulation" mode option can be deactivated.
The Modbus TCP protocol is used for the application layer of the connection.
Communication settings involve specifying the destination IP address (IPv4, IPv6, or hostname) for the TCP/IP connection.
Advanced communication options include write permissions, a Read/Write Port (default 502), and the connection type (Client/Active).
The SME IP Address (Destination Address) can be viewed or configured using the RCP software.
warnings_notes: The connection name must be unique throughout the whole configuration.
The address format and advanced options for the IP address can be left as shown in Figure 6.
The destination IP address can be specified as an IP address V4, V6, or a hostname.
The 'Modify' button is available for more advanced configuration options regarding communication settings.

key_facts: Rheonics' factory default slave address for the sensor is 255.
The PLC Protocol Settings require the start address to be 0.
Checking 'Use Word Swap' enables 32-bit double words.
Checking 'Use Item Unit ID' allows the slave address to be specified via item syntax.
The connection protocol used is Modbus TCP.
Read optimization is activated with a bytes gap of 252 bytes.
Cyclic read is activated with a read interval of 1000 ms for server send read requests to the PLC.
A successful connection test confirms communication with 'Rheonics_1' via Modbus TCP at destination address 10.5.33.84, using read/write port 502.

There are three categories for variable type when a new item is added: root, node, and tag. Root (in the example: Rheonics_1) has the connection name and can’t be modified.

Figure 11: Create a new Node item.

11. Then, create a Tag type Item and name it. The Tag Syntax needs to be specified. For this example, set the Syntax to ERR40. Click Ok, note that the Data Type is updated after clicking ok.
key_facts: The Address Space Definition window displays a connection's data items in a tree view.
New items can be created by pressing the 'Add a new Item' button (Ctrl+A).
The connection 'Rheonics_1' uses the Modbus TCP protocol.
PLC items can be defined manually or imported from a Wago project or an external file.
Variable types for new items include root, node, and tag.
The root item (e.g., Rheonics_1) uses the connection name and cannot be modified.
A Node type item can have any type of children.
A Tag type item cannot have any children.
The Tag Syntax needs to be specified when creating a Tag type item, for example, setting it to ERR40 updates the Data Type.
warnings_notes: Type Node can have any type of children, class Tag can not have any children.
Root (in the example: Rheonics_1) has the connection name and can’t be modified.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides detailed instructions and syntax definitions for configuring Modbus items within a 'Controller / Device Connection Wizard' interface, focusing on data types and addressing for Rheonics devices.
chunks.extracted_text:
Controller / Device Connection Wizard
Add PLC Item Properties
On this Item Data
Connec
Type
Specify the basic type for the actual PLC data item. Type Node can have
any type of children, class Tag can not have any children.
Rhet
16 Type: Tag
Data Item
Connection Settings
Specify the item and the Syntax of the actual Item.
Name: Viscosity
ID: Rheonics_1.Test1.Viscosity
17
Communication Settings
Syntax: ERR40
18
PLC Protocol Settings
Access Rights: Read and Write
Extended Settings
Data Type: ANY (Any)
Address Space
Definition
19 OK
Cancel
Help
< Back
Finish
Figure 12: Create a new Tag item.
The Modbus Item syntax has the following order:
[UnitID.][Group.]<Area><Data type><Start address>.<Bit number>[.Array size][Suffix]
Legend: < > Mandatory [ ] Optional
Figure 13: Modbus item syntax (dataFEED User Manual).
• <Area> defines where the data is received from with an Abbreviation. These are the following:
Table 1: Area definition for data syntax.
Syntax Abbreviation | Abbreviation Number | Orientation | Access right
Discrete Inputs | I, E, DI or DE | 1xxxxx | Bit | Read
Discrete Outputs | A, O, Q, DA, DO or DQ | 0xxxxx | Bit | Read/Write
Input Register | ER or IR | 3xxxxx | Word | Read
Register | R | 4xxxxx | Word | Read/Write
Holding Register | HR | 4xxxxx | Word | Read/Write
• <Data type> defines the variable type with the following syntax.
Table 2: Data Type definition for data syntax.
Bit | Int | Word | DInt | DWord | Real | Double | String
X | I | W | D, DI | DW | R | RD | S
• Start address specifies the starting address at which the read or write will start. For Rheonics SME the measurement values start at address 40.
• Bit number must be specified when the data type is bool.
In this case, the syntax ERR40 means: Parameters come from an Input Register, are Real (Float) type and start from address 40.
12. The hierarchy tree should look like the figure below. With this, we have created one tag that points to address 40 from the Input Register, which is Viscosity Median. Some of the Registers, Parameters and its address can be found in Rheonics Support Page (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000297664-modbus-tcp-input-registers).
key_facts: The page illustrates the creation of a new Tag item in a 'PLC Item Properties' dialog, specifically setting its Name to 'Viscosity' and Syntax to 'ERR40'.
Modbus Item syntax follows the order: `[UnitID.][Group.]<Area><Data type><Start address>.<Bit number>[.Array size][Suffix]`, where '< >' denotes mandatory components and '[ ]' optional ones.

The '<Area>' component of the Modbus syntax defines the data source, with categories like Discrete Inputs (Read, Bit), Discrete Outputs (Read/Write, Bit), Input Register (Read, Word), Register (Read/Write, Word), and Holding Register (Read/Write, Word).
The '<Data type>' component defines the variable type, offering options such as Bit (X), Int (I), Word (W), DInt (D, DI), DWord (DW), Real (R), Double (RD), and String (S).
For Rheonics SME devices, measurement values, including Viscosity Median, are found in the Input Register and start at Modbus address 40.
The syntax 'ERR40' implies that parameters are sourced from an Input Register, are of Real (Float) type, and commence at address 40.
A dedicated Rheonics Support Page provides further details on Modbus TCP Input Registers.

Importing Configuration Files
Instead of creating tags one by one, it is possible to import the configuration of the tag with an external Text File (.txt), including their name and syntax. Follow the next steps to do that.

Figure 16: Import tags configuration.
key_facts: The Controller / Device Connection Wizard allows for defining the address space for a connection.
The connection 'Rheonics_1' uses the Modbus TCP protocol.
PLC items (tags) can be added manually, imported from a Wago project, or imported from an external file.
The application needs to be restarted (e.g., 'Softing dataFEED OPC Suite') to apply and save changes to the configuration.
Tag configurations, including name and syntax, can be imported from an external Text File (.txt) to streamline the setup process.

Import File

key_facts: A text file containing alias items for a PLC system is imported using the PLC Alias File Import Wizard.
The import process involves selecting a file and ensuring the 'Import directly on the connection root node' option is checked.
The example text file content includes median and raw values for viscosity, density, temperature, and a sensor status.
The import wizard confirms the successful import of 7 PLC items.
The final step in the import process is clicking the 'Finish' button.
diagram_labels: 3 (Import directly on the connection root node checkbox)
Select the file name and the storage location of the local items data file to be imported.
Delete existing address space - the address space can not be overwritten because it is using at least once as data destination or data processing item.
warnings_notes: The address space cannot be overwritten if it is currently being used as a data destination or for data processing.

key_facts: The page describes how to define the address space for a Rheonics device connection using Modbus TCP protocol.
The address space includes various sensor data points such as viscosity (median5, raw), density (median5, raw), temperature (median, raw), and sensor status.
The configuration is performed within the 'Softing dataFEED OPC Suite' application.
The Rheonics device, identified as 'Rheonics_1', is configured to connect via Modbus to the IP address 10.5.33.84.
After making configuration changes, the application must be restarted to save them.
